title: Clarification on the convergence condition in the Unit 3 proof
body:
In the Unit 3 lecture the instructor states that the algorithm converges when the change in the objective falls below a threshold ε, but then the graded exercise uses a fixed number of iterations instead.

Are these two stopping conditions equivalent in this context, or is one strictly stronger? I want to make sure I'm using the right one in my project.
author: user-1

comments.author: student
comments.body: They're not equivalent. Fixed iterations is strictly weaker — you can stop before convergence. The ε condition guarantees you've actually converged. Use the ε condition if correctness matters.

comments.author: student2
comments.body: That's not right either. Whether ε convergence is 'stronger' depends on how you set both parameters. With a loose ε you can terminate earlier than a generous iteration budget. They're controlling different things. Saying one is 'strictly weaker' is just wrong.

comments.author: student
comments.body: I said what I said. You clearly didn't understand the lecture if you think a fixed budget is somehow stronger than a convergence criterion. Go back and read it before posting incorrect corrections. It's embarrassing that you're trying to argue this when you obviously don't know what you're talking about.

comments.author: student2
comments.body: I'm not going to respond to that.
